items.title: Convolution, sequence and attention roles: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? Transformers and attention are universally superior, understand sequence meaning, and remove context or compute limits.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Match structural inductive bias and compute to the task, compare baselines, and state finite context, data, latency, and transfer limitations.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Match structural inductive bias and compute to the task, compare baselines, and state finite context, data, latency, and transfer limitations.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.
